trades.sentiment_summary: Amkor Technology (AMKR) stock reached a new 52-week high following analyst upgrades, including JPMorgan raising its price target to $65 and shifting to Overweight, alongside positive Q1 revenue guidance exceeding expectations and strong AI-driven growth projections.[1] Recent earnings beat analyst forecasts with revenue up 15.9% year-over-year, prompting upward revisions in consensus EPS estimates and price targets.[2][4] Zacks highlights AMKR as a top momentum stock with a Momentum Style Score of A and shares up 15.4% in the past four weeks.[2]

Fluor Corporation (FLR) announced a partnership with Centrus Energy to serve as EPC contractor for expanding uranium enrichment capacity in Ohio, addressing a substantial LEU backlog amid rising demand. The stock rose in premarket trading, up 0.92% to $48.57, trading above key moving averages with positive technical indicators. Upcoming earnings on February 17, 2026, show lowered EPS estimates but a low P/E ratio suggesting value.

Amphenol announced a first quarter 2026 cash dividend of $0.25 per share, approved on February 4, 2026, signaling confidence in financial health[3]. Zacks highlighted strong growth prospects in AI-driven datacom and defense, justifying a premium P/E ratio despite trading above peers, with recent analyst upgrades and a Moderate Buy consensus[1][2][4]. Institutional investors like Varma Mutual increased stakes, and the stock opened positively amid acquisition updates[2][3][5].

No specific news articles about Core & Main (CNM) stock appear in the last 24 hours from February 11-12, 2026. Available data shows minor price fluctuations, with the stock trading at $57.84 on February 11 and $56.72 recently, alongside a 1.65% decline noted on the investor relations page. General market news indicates U.S. stocks closed slightly lower amid mixed economic signals.

Coeur Mining (CDE) is trading around $23 amid a proposed acquisition of New Gold Inc. to create a larger North American precious metals producer, with positive exploration updates at Palmarejo supporting resource growth.[5] A recent valuation analysis estimates fair value at $21.60, suggesting the stock at $23.08 trades slightly above intrinsic value despite strong momentum from gold and silver rallies.[2] Pre-market data shows trading at $22.82, with improving earnings estimates and a consensus 'Buy' analyst rating.[1][3][4]

LSV Asset Management acquired 368,100 shares of Albertsons Companies (ACI), while Sound Shore Management holds $85.84 million in the stock, signaling institutional interest on February 12, 2026. A technical analysis report highlights mixed sentiment with choppy conditions, neutral near-term signals, and both long and short trading strategies. Recent analyst consensus remains a Hold with a $21.58 target price, amid prior earnings beats but ongoing stock weakness.

Barrick Mining (NYSE: B) reported strong Q4 earnings on February 5th, beating analyst estimates with $1.04 EPS versus $0.85 expected and $5.98B in revenue versus $5.15B anticipated. Multiple analyst upgrades this week include Stifel raising its price target to C$95 with a Buy rating and ATB Cormark upgrading to 'moderate buy,' though Citi raised its price target to $48 while maintaining a Neutral rating, citing execution concerns around announced strategic moves.

Credo Technology Group (CRDO) stock surged 9.2% to $134.72 and up 17% in after-hours/premarket after announcing preliminary Q3 FY2026 revenue of $404-408 million, far exceeding prior guidance of $335-345 million and consensus estimates.[1][2][4] The beat is driven by strong AI/data-center demand, prompting analyst upgrades including Needham's Buy rating with $220 target and others up to $240.[3][6] Expected Q3 EPS of $0.78 (+212% YoY) and revenue growth over 150% fuel optimism despite high valuation concerns.[1][2]

Match Group (MTCH) reported strong Q4 2025 earnings with an earnings beat, updated 2026 guidance, a dividend hike to $0.20 quarterly (2.37% yield), and completion of a significant buyback program, alongside positive Tinder engagement trends.[1][4][5] Analysts maintain a consensus 'Hold' rating with an average price target of $35.86, above the recent price around $31.37-$31.64, while Simply Wall St views the stock as 16% undervalued at a fair value of $37.37 based on margin improvements from payment optimizations.[1][2] Share price showed short-term momentum with a 7-day return of 2.55% and a slight intraday gain of 0.76%, despite a 3.5% drop noted at open.[1][2][3]

Axalta Coating Systems reported record full-year 2025 earnings with strong Adjusted EBITDA margins and free cash flow on February 10, 2026, while issuing Q1 2026 guidance for net sales growth and $240-250 million Adjusted EBITDA[1][2]. The company continues advancing its all-stock merger of equals with AkzoNobel, announced in November 2025, expected to close in late 2026 or early 2027, promising synergies and value creation[2][3]. A passive investor increased its stake to 8.18% as of December 31, 2025[4].

Ally Financial declared a $0.30 quarterly dividend payable February 17, 2026, alongside dividends on preferred securities, demonstrating continued capital distribution strength.[3] The company's stock trades at an attractive valuation with forward P/E of 8.12X below the industry average, supported by expected 13% revenue growth in 2026 and improving net financing revenues from balance sheet repositioning actions undertaken in March 2025.[1]

No significant news articles about Banc of California (BANC) stock were published in the last 24 hours as of February 12, 2026, 4 PM UTC. The most recent items include a large insider sale by Warburg Pincus on February 2, 2026, totaling $237 million at $20.00 per share, and earlier positive earnings reports from early February with analyst upgrades.[2][1]

Citigroup declared a quarterly common stock dividend of $0.60 per share, payable on February 27, 2026, to stockholders of record on February 2, 2026[4]. Impax Asset Management Group reduced its position in Citigroup shares by 13.6% during the third quarter[1]. No other major Citigroup-specific news events reported in the last 24 hours.
